21	//
22	// Instruction pipeline L1 data cache data stage.
23	// - Detects cache miss or hit based on tag information fetched from last
24	//   stage.
25	// - Reads from cache data storage.
26	// - Drives signals to previous stage to update LRU
27	//
28	
29	module dcache_data_stage(
30	    input                                     clk,
31	    input                                     reset,
32	
33	    // To instruction_decode_stage
34	    output local_thread_bitmap_t              dd_load_sync_pending,
35	
36	    // From dcache_tag_stage
37	    input                                     dt_instruction_valid,
38	    input decoded_instruction_t               dt_instruction,
39	    input vector_mask_t                       dt_mask_value,
40	    input local_thread_idx_t                  dt_thread_idx,
41	    input l1d_addr_t                          dt_request_vaddr,
42	    input l1d_addr_t                          dt_request_paddr,
43	    input                                     dt_tlb_hit,
44	    input                                     dt_tlb_present,
45	    input                                     dt_tlb_supervisor,
46	    input                                     dt_tlb_writable,
47	    input vector_t                            dt_store_value,
48	    input subcycle_t                          dt_subcycle,
49	    input                                     dt_valid[`L1D_WAYS],
50	    input l1d_tag_t                           dt_tag[`L1D_WAYS],
51	
52	    // To dcache_tag_stage
53	    output logic                              dd_update_lru_en,
54	    output l1d_way_idx_t                      dd_update_lru_way,
55	
56	    // To io_request_queue
57	    output logic                              dd_io_write_en,
58	    output logic                              dd_io_read_en,
59	    output local_thread_idx_t                 dd_io_thread_idx,
60	    output scalar_t                           dd_io_addr,
61	    output scalar_t                           dd_io_write_value,
62	
63	    // To writeback_stage
64	    output logic                              dd_instruction_valid,
65	    output decoded_instruction_t              dd_instruction,
66	    output vector_mask_t                      dd_lane_mask,
67	    output local_thread_idx_t                 dd_thread_idx,
68	    output l1d_addr_t                         dd_request_vaddr,
69	    output subcycle_t                         dd_subcycle,
70	    output logic                              dd_rollback_en,
71	    output scalar_t                           dd_rollback_pc,
72	    output cache_line_data_t                  dd_load_data,
73	    output logic                              dd_suspend_thread,
74	    output logic                              dd_io_access,
75	    output logic                              dd_trap,
76	    output trap_cause_t                       dd_trap_cause,
77	
78	    // From control registers
79	    input logic                               cr_supervisor_en[`THREADS_PER_CORE],
80	
81	    // To control_registers
82	    // These signals are unregistered
83	    output logic                              dd_creg_write_en,
84	    output logic                              dd_creg_read_en,
85	    output control_register_t                 dd_creg_index,
86	    output scalar_t                           dd_creg_write_val,
87	
88	    // From l1_l2_interface
89	    input                                     l2i_ddata_update_en,
90	    input l1d_way_idx_t                       l2i_ddata_update_way,
91	    input l1d_set_idx_t                       l2i_ddata_update_set,
92	    input cache_line_data_t                   l2i_ddata_update_data,
93	    input [`L1D_WAYS - 1:0]                   l2i_dtag_update_en_oh,
94	    input l1d_set_idx_t                       l2i_dtag_update_set,
95	    input l1d_tag_t                           l2i_dtag_update_tag,
96	
97	     // To l1_l2_interface
98	    output logic                              dd_cache_miss,
99	    output cache_line_index_t                 dd_cache_miss_addr,
100	    output local_thread_idx_t                 dd_cache_miss_thread_idx,
101	    output logic                              dd_cache_miss_sync,
102	    output logic                              dd_store_en,
103	    output logic                              dd_flush_en,
104	    output logic                              dd_membar_en,
105	    output logic                              dd_iinvalidate_en,
106	    output logic                              dd_dinvalidate_en,
107	    output logic[CACHE_LINE_BYTES - 1:0]      dd_store_mask,
108	    output cache_line_index_t                 dd_store_addr,
109	    output cache_line_data_t                  dd_store_data,
110	    output local_thread_idx_t                 dd_store_thread_idx,
111	    output logic                              dd_store_sync,
112	    output cache_line_index_t                 dd_store_bypass_addr,
113	    output local_thread_idx_t                 dd_store_bypass_thread_idx,
114	
115	    // From writeback_stage
116	    input logic                               wb_rollback_en,
117	    input local_thread_idx_t                  wb_rollback_thread_idx,
118	    input pipeline_sel_t                      wb_rollback_pipeline,
119	
120	    // To performance_counters
121	    output logic                              dd_perf_dcache_hit,
122	    output logic                              dd_perf_dcache_miss,
123	    output logic                              dd_perf_dtlb_miss);
124	
125	    logic memory_access_req;
126	    logic cached_access_req;
127	    logic cached_load_req;
128	    logic cached_store_req;
129	    logic creg_access_req;
130	    logic io_access_req;
131	    logic sync_access_req;
132	    logic cache_control_req;
133	    logic tlb_update_req;
134	    logic flush_req;
135	    logic iinvalidate_req;
136	    logic dinvalidate_req;
137	    logic membar_req;
138	    logic addr_in_io_region;
139	    logic unaligned_address;
140	    logic supervisor_fault;
141	    logic alignment_fault;
142	    logic privileged_op_fault;
143	    logic write_fault;
144	    logic tlb_miss;
145	    logic page_fault;
146	    logic any_fault;
147	    vector_mask_t word_store_mask;
148	    logic[3:0] byte_store_mask;
149	    logic[$clog2(CACHE_LINE_WORDS) - 1:0] cache_lane_idx;
150	    cache_line_data_t endian_twiddled_data;
151	    scalar_t lane_store_value;
152	    logic[CACHE_LINE_WORDS - 1:0] cache_lane_mask;
153	    logic[CACHE_LINE_WORDS - 1:0] subcycle_mask;
154	    logic[`L1D_WAYS - 1:0] way_hit_oh;
155	    l1d_way_idx_t way_hit_idx;
156	    logic cache_hit;
157	    scalar_t dcache_request_addr;
158	    logic squash_instruction;
159	    logic cache_near_miss;
160	    logic[$clog2(NUM_VECTOR_LANES) - 1:0] scgath_lane;
161	    logic tlb_read;
162	    logic fault_store_flag;
163	    logic lane_enabled;
164	
165	    // Unlike earlier stages, this commits instruction side effects like stores,
166	    // so it needs to check if there is a rollback (which would be for the
167	    // instruction issued immediately before this one) and avoid updates if so.
168	    // squash_instruction indicates a rollback is requested from the previous
169	    // instruction, but it does not get set when this stage requests a rollback.
170	    assign squash_instruction = wb_rollback_en
171	        && wb_rollback_thread_idx == dt_thread_idx
172	        && wb_rollback_pipeline == PIPE_MEM;
173	    assign scgath_lane = ~dt_subcycle;
174	
175	    // If a scatter/gather is active, need to check if the lane is active.
176	    // This is more than an optimization: if the lane is masked, we need to
177	    // ignore the pointer to not raise a fault if it is invalid.
178	    idx_to_oh #(
179	        .NUM_SIGNALS(CACHE_LINE_WORDS),
180	        .DIRECTION("LSB0")
181	    ) idx_to_oh_subcycle(
182	        .one_hot(subcycle_mask),
183	        .index(dt_subcycle));
184	
185	    assign lane_enabled = !dt_instruction.memory_access
186	        || dt_instruction.memory_access_type != MEM_SCGATH_M
187	        || (dt_mask_value & subcycle_mask) != 0;
188	
189	    // Decode request type. If this instruction is squashed, ignore (same as
190	    // dt_instruction_valid being false). These do not consider if the
191	    // request is legal or possible, just what the instruction is asking to do.
192	    assign addr_in_io_region = dt_request_paddr ==? 32'hffff????;
193	    assign sync_access_req = dt_instruction.memory_access_type == MEM_SYNC;
194	
195	    // Note the last part that checks the store mask. If the store mask is zero
196	    // don't mark this as a memory access request. This is more than an optimization:
197	    // when performing a scatter store,
198	    assign memory_access_req = dt_instruction_valid
199	        && !squash_instruction
200	        && dt_instruction.memory_access
201	        && dt_instruction.memory_access_type != MEM_CONTROL_REG
202	        && lane_enabled;
203	    assign io_access_req = memory_access_req && addr_in_io_region;
204	    assign cached_access_req = memory_access_req && !addr_in_io_region;
205	    assign cached_load_req = cached_access_req && dt_instruction.load;
206	    assign cached_store_req = cached_access_req && !dt_instruction.load;
207	    assign cache_control_req = dt_instruction_valid
208	        && !squash_instruction
209	        && dt_instruction.cache_control;
210	    assign flush_req = cache_control_req
211	        && dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_DFLUSH
212	        && !addr_in_io_region;
213	    assign iinvalidate_req = cache_control_req
214	        && dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_IINVALIDATE
215	        && !addr_in_io_region;
216	    assign dinvalidate_req = cache_control_req
217	        && dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_DINVALIDATE
218	        && !addr_in_io_region;
219	    assign membar_req = cache_control_req
220	        && dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_MEMBAR;
221	    assign tlb_update_req = cache_control_req
222	        && (dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_DTLB_INSERT
223	            || dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_ITLB_INSERT
224	            || dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_TLB_INVAL
225	            || dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_TLB_INVAL_ALL);
226	    assign creg_access_req = dt_instruction_valid
227	        && !squash_instruction
228	        && dt_instruction.memory_access
229	        && dt_instruction.memory_access_type == MEM_CONTROL_REG;
230	
231	    // Determine if this instruction accessed the TLB (and thus possibly
232	    // missed it)
233	    always_comb
234	    begin
235	        tlb_read = 0;
236	        if (dt_instruction_valid && !squash_instruction)
237	        begin
238	            if (dt_instruction.memory_access)
239	            begin
240	                tlb_read = dt_instruction.memory_access_type != MEM_CONTROL_REG
241	                    && lane_enabled;
242	            end
243	            else if (dt_instruction.cache_control)
244	            begin
245	                // Only these cache control opertions perform a virtual->physical
246	                // address translation.
247	                tlb_read = dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_DFLUSH
248	                    || dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_DINVALIDATE;
249	            end
250	        end
251	    end
252	
253	    assign tlb_miss = tlb_read && !dt_tlb_hit;
254	
255	    // Check for faults. This will set a fault type, and the signal 'any_fault', which
256	    // will cause side effects of subsequent memory operations to be disabled. Each
257	    // of this is only true if the operation is actually requested.
258	    always_comb
259	    begin
260	        unique case (dt_instruction.memory_access_type)
261	            MEM_S, MEM_SX: unaligned_address = dt_request_paddr.offset[0];
262	            MEM_L, MEM_SYNC, MEM_SCGATH, MEM_SCGATH_M: unaligned_address = |dt_request_paddr.offset[1:0];
263	            MEM_BLOCK, MEM_BLOCK_M: unaligned_address = dt_request_paddr.offset != 0;
264	            default: unaligned_address = 0;
265	        endcase
266	    end
267	
268	    assign alignment_fault = (cached_access_req || io_access_req) && unaligned_address;
269	    assign privileged_op_fault = (creg_access_req || tlb_update_req || dinvalidate_req)
270	        && !cr_supervisor_en[dt_thread_idx];
271	    assign page_fault = memory_access_req
272	        && dt_tlb_hit
273	        && !dt_tlb_present;
274	    assign supervisor_fault = memory_access_req
275	        && dt_tlb_hit
276	        && dt_tlb_present
277	        && dt_tlb_supervisor
278	        && !cr_supervisor_en[dt_thread_idx];
279	    assign write_fault = (cached_store_req || (io_access_req && !dt_instruction.load))
280	        && dt_tlb_hit
281	        && dt_tlb_present
282	        && !supervisor_fault
283	        && !dt_tlb_writable;
284	    assign any_fault = alignment_fault || privileged_op_fault || page_fault
285	        || supervisor_fault || write_fault;
286	
287	    // *** Everything below this point needs to check tlb_miss (if applicable)
288	    // and any_fault before enabling operations. ***
289	
290	    // L1 data cache or store buffer access
291	    assign dd_store_en = cached_store_req
292	        && !tlb_miss
293	        && !any_fault;
294	    assign dcache_request_addr = {dt_request_paddr[31:CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH],
295	        {CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH{1'b0}}};
296	    assign cache_lane_idx = dt_request_paddr.offset[CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH - 1:2];
297	    assign dd_store_bypass_addr = dt_request_paddr[31:CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH];
298	    assign dd_store_bypass_thread_idx = dt_thread_idx;
299	    assign dd_store_addr = dt_request_paddr[31:CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH];
300	    assign dd_store_sync = sync_access_req;
301	    assign dd_store_thread_idx = dt_thread_idx;
302	
303	    // Noncached I/O memory access
304	    assign dd_io_write_en = io_access_req
305	        && !dt_instruction.load
306	        && !tlb_miss
307	        && !any_fault;
308	    assign dd_io_read_en = io_access_req
309	        && dt_instruction.load
310	        && !tlb_miss
311	        && !any_fault;
312	    assign dd_io_write_value = dt_store_value[0];
313	    assign dd_io_thread_idx = dt_thread_idx;
314	    assign dd_io_addr = {16'd0, dt_request_paddr[15:0]};
315	
316	    // Control register access
317	    assign dd_creg_write_en = creg_access_req
318	        && !dt_instruction.load
319	        && !any_fault;
320	    assign dd_creg_read_en = creg_access_req
321	        && dt_instruction.load
322	        && !any_fault;
323	    assign dd_creg_write_val = dt_store_value[0];
324	    assign dd_creg_index = dt_instruction.creg_index;
325	
326	    // Cache control
327	    // Unlike other operations, these check dt_tlb_present, as these will not raise
328	    // a fault if the page is not present.
329	    assign dd_flush_en = flush_req
330	        && dt_tlb_hit
331	        && dt_tlb_present
332	        && !io_access_req // XXX should a cache control of IO address raise exception?
333	        && !any_fault;
334	    assign dd_iinvalidate_en = iinvalidate_req
335	        && dt_tlb_hit
336	        && dt_tlb_present
337	        && !io_access_req
338	        && !any_fault;
339	    assign dd_dinvalidate_en = dinvalidate_req
340	        && dt_tlb_hit
341	        && dt_tlb_present
342	        && !io_access_req
343	        && !any_fault;
344	    assign dd_membar_en = membar_req
345	        && dt_instruction.cache_control_op == CACHE_MEMBAR;
346	
347	    //
348	    // Check for cache hit
349	    //
350	    genvar way_idx;
351	    generate
352	        for (way_idx = 0; way_idx < `L1D_WAYS; way_idx++)
353	        begin : hit_check_gen
354	            assign way_hit_oh[way_idx] = dt_request_paddr.tag == dt_tag[way_idx]
355	                && dt_valid[way_idx];
356	        end
357	    endgenerate
358	
359	    // Treat a synchronized load as a cache miss the first time it occurs, because
360	    // it needs to send it to the L2 cache to register it.
361	    assign cache_hit = |way_hit_oh
362	        && (!sync_access_req || dd_load_sync_pending[dt_thread_idx])
363	        && dt_tlb_hit;
364	
365	    //
366	    // Store alignment
367	    //
368	    idx_to_oh #(
369	        .NUM_SIGNALS(CACHE_LINE_WORDS),
370	        .DIRECTION("LSB0")
371	    ) idx_to_oh_cache_lane(
372	        .one_hot(cache_lane_mask),
373	        .index(cache_lane_idx));
374	
375	    always_comb
376	    begin
377	        word_store_mask = 0;
378	        unique case (dt_instruction.memory_access_type)
379	            MEM_BLOCK, MEM_BLOCK_M:    // Block vector access
380	                word_store_mask = dt_mask_value;
381	
382	            MEM_SCGATH, MEM_SCGATH_M:    // Scatter/Gather access
383	            begin
384	                if ((dt_mask_value & subcycle_mask) != 0)
385	                    word_store_mask = cache_lane_mask;
386	                else
387	                    word_store_mask = 0;
388	            end
389	
390	            default:    // Scalar access
391	                word_store_mask = cache_lane_mask;
392	        endcase
393	    end
394	
395	    // Endian swap vector data
396	    genvar swap_word;
397	    generate
398	        for (swap_word = 0; swap_word < CACHE_LINE_BYTES / 4; swap_word++)
399	        begin : swap_word_gen
400	            assign endian_twiddled_data[swap_word * 32+:8] = dt_store_value[swap_word][24+:8];
401	            assign endian_twiddled_data[swap_word * 32 + 8+:8] = dt_store_value[swap_word][16+:8];
402	            assign endian_twiddled_data[swap_word * 32 + 16+:8] = dt_store_value[swap_word][8+:8];
403	            assign endian_twiddled_data[swap_word * 32 + 24+:8] = dt_store_value[swap_word][0+:8];
404	        end
405	    endgenerate
406	
407	    assign lane_store_value = dt_store_value[scgath_lane];
408	
409	    // byte_store_mask and dd_store_data.
410	    always_comb
411	    begin
412	        unique case (dt_instruction.memory_access_type)
413	            MEM_B, MEM_BX: // Byte
414	            begin
415	                dd_store_data = {CACHE_LINE_WORDS * 4{dt_store_value[0][7:0]}};
416	                case (dt_request_paddr.offset[1:0])
417	                    2'd0: byte_store_mask = 4'b1000;
418	                    2'd1: byte_store_mask = 4'b0100;
419	                    2'd2: byte_store_mask = 4'b0010;
420	                    2'd3: byte_store_mask = 4'b0001;
421	                    default: byte_store_mask = 4'b0000;
422	                endcase
423	            end
424	
425	            MEM_S, MEM_SX: // 16 bits
426	            begin
427	                dd_store_data = {CACHE_LINE_WORDS * 2{dt_store_value[0][7:0], dt_store_value[0][15:8]}};
428	                if (dt_request_paddr.offset[1] == 1'b0)
429	                    byte_store_mask = 4'b1100;
430	                else
431	                    byte_store_mask = 4'b0011;
432	            end
433	
434	            MEM_L, MEM_SYNC: // 32 bits
435	            begin
436	                byte_store_mask = 4'b1111;
437	                dd_store_data = {CACHE_LINE_WORDS{dt_store_value[0][7:0], dt_store_value[0][15:8],
438	                    dt_store_value[0][23:16], dt_store_value[0][31:24]}};
439	            end
440	
441	            MEM_SCGATH, MEM_SCGATH_M:
442	            begin
443	                byte_store_mask = 4'b1111;
444	                dd_store_data = {CACHE_LINE_WORDS{lane_store_value[7:0], lane_store_value[15:8],
445	                    lane_store_value[23:16], lane_store_value[31:24]}};
446	            end
447	
448	            default: // Vector
449	            begin
450	                byte_store_mask = 4'b1111;
451	                dd_store_data = endian_twiddled_data;
452	            end
453	        endcase
454	    end
455	
456	    // Generate store mask signals. word_store_mask corresponds to lanes,
457	    // byte_store_mask corresponds to bytes within a word. byte_store_mask
458	    // always has all bits set if word_store_mask has more than one bit set:
459	    // either select some number of words within the cache line for
460	    // a vector transfer or some bytes within a word for a scalar transfer.
461	    genvar mask_idx;
462	    generate
463	        for (mask_idx = 0; mask_idx < CACHE_LINE_BYTES; mask_idx++)
464	        begin : store_mask_gen
465	            assign dd_store_mask[mask_idx] = word_store_mask[
466	                (CACHE_LINE_BYTES - mask_idx - 1) / 4]
467	                & byte_store_mask[mask_idx & 3];
468	        end
469	    endgenerate
470	
471	    oh_to_idx #(.NUM_SIGNALS(`L1D_WAYS)) encode_hit_way(
472	        .one_hot(way_hit_oh),
473	        .index(way_hit_idx));
474	
475	    sram_1r1w #(
476	        .DATA_WIDTH(CACHE_LINE_BITS),
477	        .SIZE(`L1D_WAYS * `L1D_SETS),
478	        .READ_DURING_WRITE("NEW_DATA")
479	    ) l1d_data(
480	        // Instruction pipeline access.
481	        .read_en(cache_hit && cached_load_req),
482	        .read_addr({way_hit_idx, dt_request_paddr.set_idx}),
483	        .read_data(dd_load_data),
484	
485	        // Update from L2 cache interface
486	        .write_en(l2i_ddata_update_en),
487	        .write_addr({l2i_ddata_update_way, l2i_ddata_update_set}),
488	        .write_data(l2i_ddata_update_data),
489	        .*);
490	
491	    // cache_near_miss indicates a cache miss is occurring in the cycle this is
492	    // filling the same line. If this suspends the thread, it will never
493	    // receive a wakeup. Instead, roll the thread back and let it retry.
494	    // Do not be set for a synchronized load, even if the data is in the L1
495	    // cache: it must do a round trip to the L2 cache to latch the address.
496	    assign cache_near_miss = !cache_hit
497	        && dt_tlb_hit
498	        && cached_load_req
499	        && |l2i_dtag_update_en_oh
500	        && l2i_dtag_update_set == dt_request_paddr.set_idx
501	        && l2i_dtag_update_tag == dt_request_paddr.tag
502	        && !sync_access_req
503	        && !any_fault;
504	
505	    assign dd_cache_miss = cached_load_req
506	        && !cache_hit
507	        && dt_tlb_hit
508	        && !cache_near_miss
509	        && !any_fault;
510	    assign dd_cache_miss_addr = dcache_request_addr[31:CACHE_LINE_OFFSET_WIDTH];
511	    assign dd_cache_miss_thread_idx = dt_thread_idx;
512	    assign dd_cache_miss_sync = sync_access_req;
513	
514	    assign dd_update_lru_en = cache_hit && cached_access_req && !any_fault;
515	    assign dd_update_lru_way = way_hit_idx;
516	
517	    // Always treat the first synchronized load as a cache miss, even if data is
518	    // present. This is to register request with L2 cache. The second request will
519	    // not be a miss if the data is in the cache (there is a window where it could
520	    // be evicted before the thread can fetch it, in which case it will retry.
521	    // load_sync_pending tracks if this is the first or second request.
522	    //
523	    // Interrupts are different than rollbacks because they can occur in the middle
524	    // of a synchronized load/store. Detect these and cancel the operation.
525	    genvar thread_idx;
526	    generate
527	        for (thread_idx = 0; thread_idx < `THREADS_PER_CORE; thread_idx++)
528	        begin : sync_pending_gen
529	            always_ff @(posedge clk, posedge reset)
530	            begin
531	                if (reset)
532	                    dd_load_sync_pending[thread_idx] <= 0;
533	                else if (cached_load_req && sync_access_req && dt_thread_idx == local_thread_idx_t'(thread_idx))
534	                begin
535	                    // Track if this is the first or restarted request.
536	                    dd_load_sync_pending[thread_idx] <= !dd_load_sync_pending[thread_idx];
537	                end
538	            end
539	        end
540	    endgenerate
541	
542	    assign fault_store_flag = dt_instruction.memory_access
543	        && !dt_instruction.load;
544	
545	    always_ff @(posedge clk)
546	    begin
547	        dd_instruction <= dt_instruction;
548	        dd_lane_mask <= dt_mask_value;
549	        dd_thread_idx <= dt_thread_idx;
550	        dd_request_vaddr <= dt_request_vaddr;
551	        dd_subcycle <= dt_subcycle;
552	        dd_rollback_pc <= dt_instruction.pc;
553	        dd_io_access <= io_access_req;
554	
555	        // Check for TLB miss first, since permission bits are not valid if
556	        // there is a TLB miss. The order of the remaining items should match
557	        // that in instruction_decode_stage for consistency.
558	        if (tlb_miss)
559	            dd_trap_cause <= {1'b1, fault_store_flag, TT_TLB_MISS};
560	        else if (page_fault)
561	            dd_trap_cause <= {1'b1, fault_store_flag, TT_PAGE_FAULT};
562	        else if (supervisor_fault)
563	            dd_trap_cause <= {1'b1, fault_store_flag, TT_SUPERVISOR_ACCESS};
564	        else if (alignment_fault)
565	            dd_trap_cause <= {1'b1, fault_store_flag, TT_UNALIGNED_ACCESS};
566	        else if (privileged_op_fault)
567	            dd_trap_cause <= {2'b00, TT_PRIVILEGED_OP};
568	        else // write fault
569	            dd_trap_cause <= {2'b11, TT_ILLEGAL_STORE};
570	    end
571	
572	    always_ff @(posedge clk, posedge reset)
573	    begin
574	        if (reset)
575	        begin
576	            /*AUTORESET*/
577	            // Beginning of autoreset for uninitialized flops
578	            dd_instruction_valid <= '0;
579	            dd_perf_dcache_hit <= '0;
580	            dd_perf_dcache_miss <= '0;
581	            dd_perf_dtlb_miss <= '0;
582	            dd_rollback_en <= '0;
583	            dd_suspend_thread <= '0;
584	            dd_trap <= '0;
585	            // End of automatics
586	        end
587	        else
588	        begin
589	            // Make sure data is not present in more than one way.
590	            assert(!cached_load_req || $onehot0(way_hit_oh));
591	
592	            // Make sure this decodes only one type of instruction
593	            assert($onehot0({cached_load_req, cached_store_req, io_access_req,
594	                flush_req, iinvalidate_req, dinvalidate_req,
595	                membar_req, tlb_update_req, creg_access_req}));
596	
597	            dd_instruction_valid <= dt_instruction_valid
598	                && !squash_instruction;
599	
600	            // Rollback on cache miss
601	            dd_rollback_en <= cached_load_req && !cache_hit && dt_tlb_hit && !any_fault;
602	
603	            // Suspend the thread if there is a cache miss.
604	            // In the near miss case (described above), don't suspend thread.
605	            dd_suspend_thread <= cached_load_req
606	                && dt_tlb_hit
607	                && !cache_hit
608	                && !cache_near_miss
609	                && !any_fault;
610	
611	            dd_trap <= any_fault || tlb_miss;
612	
613	            // Perf events
614	            dd_perf_dcache_hit <= cached_load_req && !any_fault && !tlb_miss
615	                 && cache_hit;
616	            dd_perf_dcache_miss <= cached_load_req && !any_fault && !tlb_miss
617	                && !cache_hit;
618	            dd_perf_dtlb_miss <= tlb_miss;
619	        end
620	    end
621	endmodule
622

content.content:
1	//
2	// Copyright 2011-2015 Jeff Bush
3	//
4	// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	// You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	//
8	//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	//
10	// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	// distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	// limitations under the License.
15	//
16	
17	#include "asm_macros.h"
18	
19	//
20	// Verify atomic operations work correctly. Threads cycle through incrementing
21	// a series of array values. Each thread increments a shared counter atomically
22	// to find the next slot to increment, then uses an atomic operation to increment
23	// that slot.
24	//
25	                    #define NUM_SLOTS 512
26	
27	// Clobbers s25
28	.macro sync_fetch_and_increment oldval, ptr
29	1:  load_sync s25, (\ptr )
30	    move \oldval, s25
31	    add_i s25, s25, 1
32	    store_sync s25, (\ptr )
33	    bz s25, 1b
34	.endm
35	
36	                    .globl _start
37	_start:             start_all_threads
38	
39	                    li s0, (NUM_SLOTS * 10)       // Total writes
40	                    lea s1, current_index               // Pointer to shared index
41	                    li s2, 0x100000             // Pointer to array base
42	
43	fill_loop:          sync_fetch_and_increment s3, s1     // Find next slot
44	                    cmpge_i s4, s3, s0                  // Done?
45	                    bnz s4, exit_loop                 // If so, exit
46	                    and s3, s3, NUM_SLOTS - 1           // Mask so it wraps
47	                    shl s3, s3, 2                       // Multiply index by four
48	                    add_i s3, s3, s2                    // Add to array base
49	                    sync_fetch_and_increment s4, s3     // Increment array index (s4 is ignored)
50	                    b fill_loop
51	
52	exit_loop:          halt_current_thread
53	current_index:      .long 0
54

100	    OP_CMPGE_I              = 6'b010011,    // Integer greater or equal (signed)
101	    OP_CMPLT_I              = 6'b010100,    // Integer less than (signed)
102	    OP_CMPLE_I              = 6'b010101,    // Integer less than or equal (signed)
103	    OP_CMPGT_U              = 6'b010110,    // Integer greater than (unsigned)
104	    OP_CMPGE_U              = 6'b010111,    // Integer greater or equal (unsigned)
105	    OP_CMPLT_U              = 6'b011000,    // Integer less than (unsigned)
106	    OP_CMPLE_U              = 6'b011001,    // Integer less than or equal (unsigned)
107	    OP_GETLANE              = 6'b011010,    // Getlane
108	    OP_FTOI                 = 6'b011011,    // Float to integer
109	    OP_RECIPROCAL           = 6'b011100,    // Reciprocal estimate
110	    OP_SEXT8                = 6'b011101,    // Sign extend 8 bit value
111	    OP_SEXT16               = 6'b011110,    // Sign extend 16 bit value
112	    OP_MULH_I               = 6'b011111,    // Signed multiply high
113	    OP_ADD_F                = 6'b100000,    // Add floating point
114	    OP_SUB_F                = 6'b100001,    // Subtract floating point
115	    OP_MUL_F                = 6'b100010,    // Multiply floating point
116	    OP_ITOF                 = 6'b101010,    // Integer to float
117	    OP_CMPGT_F              = 6'b101100,    // Floating point greater than
118	    OP_CMPLT_F              = 6'b101110,    // Floating point less than
119	    OP_CMPGE_F              = 6'b101101,    // Floating point greater or equal
120	    OP_CMPLE_F              = 6'b101111,    // Floating point less than or equal
121	    OP_CMPEQ_F              = 6'b110000,    // Floating point equal
122	    OP_CMPNE_F              = 6'b110001,    // Floating point not-equal
123	    OP_BREAKPOINT           = 6'b111110
124	} alu_op_t;
125	
126	// Operation type field encodings for memory instructions
127	typedef enum logic[3:0] {
128	    MEM_B                   = 4'b0000,  // Byte (8 bit)
129	    MEM_BX                  = 4'b0001,  // Byte, sign extended
130	    MEM_S                   = 4'b0010,  // Short (16 bit)
131	    MEM_SX                  = 4'b0011,  // Short, sign extended
132	    MEM_L                   = 4'b0100,  // Long (32 bit)
133	    MEM_SYNC                = 4'b0101,  // Synchronized
134	    MEM_CONTROL_REG         = 4'b0110,  // Control register
135	    MEM_BLOCK               = 4'b0111,  // Vector block
136	    MEM_BLOCK_M             = 4'b1000,
137	    MEM_SCGATH              = 4'b1101,  // Vector scatter/gather
138	    MEM_SCGATH_M            = 4'b1110
139	} memory_op_t;
140	
141	// Operation type field encodings for cache control instructions
142	typedef enum logic[2:0] {
143	    CACHE_DTLB_INSERT       = 3'b000,
144	    CACHE_DINVALIDATE       = 3'b001,
145	    CACHE_DFLUSH            = 3'b010,
146	    CACHE_IINVALIDATE       = 3'b011,
147	    CACHE_MEMBAR            = 3'b100,
148	    CACHE_TLB_INVAL         = 3'b101,
149	    CACHE_TLB_INVAL_ALL     = 3'b110,
150	    CACHE_ITLB_INSERT       = 3'b111
151	} cache_op_t;
152	
153	// Operation type field encodings for branch instructions
154	typedef enum logic[2:0] {
155	    BRANCH_REGISTER         = 3'b000,
156	    BRANCH_ZERO             = 3'b001,
157	    BRANCH_NOT_ZERO         = 3'b010,
158	    BRANCH_ALWAYS           = 3'b011,
159	    BRANCH_CALL_OFFSET      = 3'b100,
160	    BRANCH_CALL_REGISTER    = 3'b110,
161	    BRANCH_ERET             = 3'b111
162	} branch_type_t;
163	
164	// Control register indices
165	typedef enum logic [4:0] {
166	    CR_THREAD_ID            = 5'd0,
167	    CR_TRAP_HANDLER         = 5'd1,
168	    CR_TRAP_PC              = 5'd2,
169	    CR_TRAP_CAUSE           = 5'd3,
170	    CR_FLAGS                = 5'd4,
171	    CR_TRAP_ADDRESS         = 5'd5,
172	    CR_CYCLE_COUNT          = 5'd6,
173	    CR_TLB_MISS_HANDLER     = 5'd7,
174	    CR_SAVED_FLAGS          = 5'd8,
175	    CR_CURRENT_ASID         = 5'd9,
176	    CR_PAGE_DIR             = 5'd10,
177	    CR_SCRATCHPAD0          = 5'd11,
178	    CR_SCRATCHPAD1          = 5'd12,
179	    CR_SUBCYCLE             = 5'd13,
180	    CR_INTERRUPT_ENABLE     = 5'd14,
181	    CR_INTERRUPT_ACK        = 5'd15,
182	    CR_INTERRUPT_PENDING    = 5'd16,
183	    CR_INTERRUPT_TRIGGER    = 5'd17,
184	    CR_JTAG_DATA            = 5'd18,
185	    CR_SYSCALL_INDEX        = 5'd19,
186	    CR_SUSPEND_THREAD       = 5'd20,
187	    CR_RESUME_THREAD        = 5'd21,
188	    CR_PERF_EVENT_SELECT0   = 5'd22,
189	    CR_PERF_EVENT_SELECT1   = 5'd23,
190	    CR_PERF_EVENT_COUNT0_L  = 5'd24,
191	    CR_PERF_EVENT_COUNT0_H  = 5'd25,
192	    CR_PERF_EVENT_COUNT1_L  = 5'd26,
193	    CR_PERF_EVENT_COUNT1_H  = 5'd27
194	} control_register_t;
195	
196	// Trap type encodings
197	typedef enum logic[3:0] {
198	    TT_RESET                = 4'd0,
199	    TT_ILLEGAL_INSTRUCTION  = 4'd1,
200	    TT_PRIVILEGED_OP        = 4'd2,
201	    TT_INTERRUPT            = 4'd3,
202	    TT_SYSCALL              = 4'd4,
203	    TT_UNALIGNED_ACCESS     = 4'd5,
204	    TT_PAGE_FAULT           = 4'd6,
205	    TT_TLB_MISS             = 4'd7,
206	    TT_ILLEGAL_STORE        = 4'd8,
207	    TT_SUPERVISOR_ACCESS    = 4'd9,
208	    TT_NOT_EXECUTABLE       = 4'd10,
209	    TT_BREAKPOINT           = 4'd11
210	} trap_type_t;
211	
212	//
213	// Decoded instruction fields
214	//
215	
216	typedef enum logic [1:0] {
217	    MASK_SRC_SCALAR1,
218	    MASK_SRC_SCALAR2,
219	    MASK_SRC_ALL_ONES
220	} mask_src_t;
221	
222	typedef enum logic {
223	    OP1_SRC_VECTOR1,
224	    OP1_SRC_SCALAR1
225	} op1_src_t;
226	
227	typedef enum logic [1:0] {
228	    OP2_SRC_SCALAR2,
229	    OP2_SRC_VECTOR2,
230	    OP2_SRC_IMMEDIATE
231	} op2_src_t;
232	
233	typedef enum logic [1:0] {
234	    PIPE_MEM,
235	    PIPE_INT_ARITH,
236	    PIPE_FLOAT_ARITH
237	} pipeline_sel_t;
238	
239	typedef struct packed {
240	    logic dcache;
241	    logic store;
242	    trap_type_t trap_type;
243	} trap_cause_t;
244	
245	typedef logic[3:0] interrupt_id_t;
246	
247	typedef struct packed {
248	    scalar_t pc;
249	
250	    // Was this instruction injected by the on-chip debugger
251	    logic injected;
252	
253	    // Piggybacked exceptions
254	    logic has_trap;
255	    trap_cause_t trap_cause;
256	
257	    // Decoded instruction fields
258	    logic has_scalar1;
259	    register_idx_t scalar_sel1;
260	    logic has_scalar2;
261	    register_idx_t scalar_sel2;
262	    logic has_vector1;
263	    register_idx_t vector_sel1;
264	    logic has_vector2;
265	    register_idx_t vector_sel2;
266	    logic has_dest;
267	    logic dest_vector;
268	    register_idx_t dest_reg;
269	    alu_op_t alu_op;
270	    mask_src_t mask_src;
271	    op1_src_t op1_src;
272	    op2_src_t op2_src;
273	    logic store_value_vector;
274	    scalar_t immediate_value;
275	    logic branch;
276	    branch_type_t branch_type;
277	    logic call;
278	    pipeline_sel_t pipeline_sel;
279	    logic memory_access;

content.text: 现在让我来查看 L2 缓存的同步处理以及 L2 请求/响应数据包结构，此外还要查看 `l1_l2_interface` 中的同步路径。

content.content:
1	//
2	// Copyright 2011-2015 Jeff Bush
3	//
4	// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	// You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	//
8	//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	//
10	// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	// distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	// limitations under the License.
15	//
16	
17	`include "defines.svh"
18	
19	import defines::*;
20	
21	//
22	// L2 cache pipeline - read stage
23	// - Checks for cache hit.
24	// - Drives signals to update LRU flags in previous stage.
25	// - Reads cache memory
26	//   * If this is a restarted cache fill request and the replaced line
27	//     is dirty, reads the old data to that this will write back to
28	//     system memory.
29	//   * If this is a cache flush request and this was a cache hit, reads
30	//     the data in the line to write back.
31	//   * If this is a cache hit, reads the data in the line.
32	// - Drives signals to update dirty flags in previous stage
33	//   * If this is a flush request, clears the dirty bit
34	//   * If this is a store request, sets the dirty bit
35	// - Drives signals to update tags in prevous stage if this is a cache fill.
36	// - Tracks synchronized load/store state.
37	//
38	
39	module l2_cache_read_stage(
40	    input                                     clk,
41	    input                                     reset,
42	
43	    // From l2_cache_tag_stage
44	    input                                     l2t_request_valid,
45	    input l2req_packet_t                      l2t_request,
46	    input                                     l2t_valid[`L2_WAYS],
47	    input l2_tag_t                            l2t_tag[`L2_WAYS],
48	    input                                     l2t_dirty[`L2_WAYS],
49	    input                                     l2t_l2_fill,
50	    input                                     l2t_restarted_flush,
51	    input l2_way_idx_t                        l2t_fill_way,
52	    input cache_line_data_t                   l2t_data_from_memory,
53	
54	    // To l2_cache_tag_stage
55	    // Update metadata.
56	    output logic[`L2_WAYS - 1:0]              l2r_update_dirty_en,
57	    output l2_set_idx_t                       l2r_update_dirty_set,
58	    output logic                              l2r_update_dirty_value,
59	    output logic[`L2_WAYS - 1:0]              l2r_update_tag_en,
60	    output l2_set_idx_t                       l2r_update_tag_set,
61	    output logic                              l2r_update_tag_valid,
62	    output l2_tag_t                           l2r_update_tag_value,
63	    output logic                              l2r_update_lru_en,
64	    output l2_way_idx_t                       l2r_update_lru_hit_way,
65	
66	    // From l2_cache_update_stage
67	    input                                     l2u_write_en,
68	    input [$clog2(`L2_WAYS * `L2_SETS) - 1:0] l2u_write_addr,
69	    input cache_line_data_t                   l2u_write_data,
70	
71	    // To l2_cache_update_stage
72	    output logic                              l2r_request_valid,
73	    output l2req_packet_t                     l2r_request,
74	    output cache_line_data_t                  l2r_data,    // Also to bus interface unit
75	    output logic                              l2r_cache_hit,
76	    output logic[$clog2(`L2_WAYS * `L2_SETS) - 1:0] l2r_hit_cache_idx,
77	    output logic                              l2r_l2_fill,
78	    output logic                              l2r_restarted_flush,
79	    output cache_line_data_t                  l2r_data_from_memory,
80	    output logic                              l2r_store_sync_success,
81	
82	    // To l2_axi_bus_interface
83	    output l2_tag_t                           l2r_writeback_tag,
84	    output logic                              l2r_needs_writeback,
85	
86	    // To performance_counters
87	    output logic                              l2r_perf_l2_miss,
88	    output logic                              l2r_perf_l2_hit);
89	
90	    localparam GLOBAL_THREAD_IDX_WIDTH = $clog2(TOTAL_THREADS);
91	
92	    // Track synchronized load/stores, and determine if a synchronized store
93	    // was successful.
94	    cache_line_index_t load_sync_address[TOTAL_THREADS];
95	    logic load_sync_address_valid[TOTAL_THREADS];
96	    logic can_store_sync;
97	
98	    logic[`L2_WAYS - 1:0] hit_way_oh;
99	    logic cache_hit;
100	    l2_way_idx_t hit_way_idx;
101	    logic[$clog2(`L2_WAYS * `L2_SETS) - 1:0] read_address;
102	    logic load;
103	    logic store;
104	    logic update_dirty;
105	    logic update_tag;
106	    logic flush_first_pass;
107	    l2_way_idx_t writeback_way;
108	    logic hit_or_miss;
109	    logic dinvalidate;
110	    l2_way_idx_t tag_update_way;
111	    logic[GLOBAL_THREAD_IDX_WIDTH - 1:0] request_sync_slot;
112	
113	    assign load = l2t_request.packet_type == L2REQ_LOAD
114	        || l2t_request.packet_type == L2REQ_LOAD_SYNC;
115	    assign store = l2t_request.packet_type == L2REQ_STORE
116	        || l2t_request.packet_type == L2REQ_STORE_SYNC;
117	    assign writeback_way = l2t_request.packet_type == L2REQ_FLUSH
118	        ? hit_way_idx : l2t_fill_way;
119	    assign dinvalidate = l2t_request.packet_type == L2REQ_DINVALIDATE;
120	
121	    //
122	    // Check for cache hit
123	    //
124	    genvar way_idx;
125	    generate
126	        for (way_idx = 0; way_idx < `L2_WAYS; way_idx++)
127	        begin : hit_way_gen
128	            assign hit_way_oh[way_idx] = l2t_request.address.tag == l2t_tag[way_idx] && l2t_valid[way_idx];
129	        end
130	    endgenerate
131	
132	    assign cache_hit = |hit_way_oh && l2t_request_valid;
133	
134	    oh_to_idx #(.NUM_SIGNALS(`L2_WAYS)) oh_to_idx_hit_way(
135	        .one_hot(hit_way_oh),
136	        .index(hit_way_idx));
137	
138	    // If this is a fill, read the old (potentially dirty line) so it
139	    // can be written back. If it is a cache hit, read the line data.
140	    assign read_address = {(l2t_l2_fill ? l2t_fill_way : hit_way_idx),
141	        l2t_request.address.set_idx};
142	
143	    //
144	    // Cache memory
145	    //
146	    sram_1r1w #(
147	        .DATA_WIDTH(CACHE_LINE_BITS),
148	        .SIZE(`L2_WAYS * `L2_SETS),
149	        .READ_DURING_WRITE("NEW_DATA")
150	    ) sram_l2_data(
151	        .read_en(l2t_request_valid && (cache_hit || l2t_l2_fill)),
152	        .read_addr(read_address),
153	        .read_data(l2r_data),
154	        .write_en(l2u_write_en),
155	        .write_addr(l2u_write_addr),
156	        .write_data(l2u_write_data),
157	        .*);
158	
159	    //
160	    // Update dirty bits. If this is a fill, initialize the dirty bit to the correct
161	    // value depending on whether this is a write. If it is a cache hit, update the
162	    // dirty bit only if this is a store.
163	    //
164	    assign flush_first_pass = l2t_request.packet_type == L2REQ_FLUSH
165	        && !l2t_restarted_flush;
166	    assign update_dirty = l2t_request_valid && (l2t_l2_fill
167	        || (cache_hit && (store || flush_first_pass)));
168	    assign l2r_update_dirty_set = l2t_request.address.set_idx;
169	    assign l2r_update_dirty_value = store;    // This is zero if this is a flush
170	
171	    genvar dirty_update_idx;
172	    generate
173	        for (dirty_update_idx = 0; dirty_update_idx < `L2_WAYS; dirty_update_idx++)
174	        begin : dirty_update_gen
175	            assign l2r_update_dirty_en[dirty_update_idx] = update_dirty
176	                && (l2t_l2_fill ? l2t_fill_way == l2_way_idx_t'(dirty_update_idx)
177	                : hit_way_oh[dirty_update_idx]);
178	        end
179	    endgenerate
180	
181	    //
182	    // Update tag memory. If this is a fill, make the new line valid. If it is an
183	    // invalidate make it invalid.
184	    //
185	    assign update_tag = l2t_l2_fill || (cache_hit && dinvalidate);
186	    assign tag_update_way = l2t_l2_fill ? l2t_fill_way : hit_way_idx;
187	    genvar tag_idx;
188	    generate
189	        for (tag_idx = 0; tag_idx < `L2_WAYS; tag_idx++)
190	        begin : tag_update_gen
191	            assign l2r_update_tag_en[tag_idx] = update_tag && tag_update_way == l2_way_idx_t'(tag_idx);
192	        end
193	    endgenerate
194	
195	    assign l2r_update_tag_set = l2t_request.address.set_idx;
196	    assign l2r_update_tag_valid = !dinvalidate;
197	    assign l2r_update_tag_value = l2t_request.address.tag;
198	
199	    //
200	    // Update LRU
201	    //
202	    assign l2r_update_lru_en = cache_hit && (load || store);
203	    assign l2r_update_lru_hit_way = hit_way_idx;
204	
205	    //
206	    // Synchronized requests
207	    //
208	    assign request_sync_slot = GLOBAL_THREAD_IDX_WIDTH'({l2t_request.core, l2t_request.id});
209	    assign can_store_sync = load_sync_address[request_sync_slot]
210	        == {l2t_request.address.tag, l2t_request.address.set_idx}
211	        && load_sync_address_valid[request_sync_slot]
212	        && l2t_request.packet_type == L2REQ_STORE_SYNC;
213	
214	    // Used for perf counters below
215	    assign hit_or_miss = l2t_request_valid
216	        && (l2t_request.packet_type == L2REQ_STORE || can_store_sync
217	        || l2t_request.packet_type == L2REQ_LOAD ) && !l2t_l2_fill;
218	
219	    always_ff @(posedge clk)
220	    begin
221	        l2r_request <= l2t_request;
222	        l2r_cache_hit <= cache_hit;
223	        l2r_l2_fill <= l2t_l2_fill;
224	        l2r_writeback_tag <= l2t_tag[writeback_way];
225	        l2r_needs_writeback <= l2t_dirty[writeback_way] && l2t_valid[writeback_way];
226	        l2r_data_from_memory <= l2t_data_from_memory;
227	        l2r_hit_cache_idx <= read_address;
228	        l2r_restarted_flush <= l2t_restarted_flush;
229	    end
230	
231	    always_ff @(posedge clk, posedge reset)
232	    begin
233	        if (reset)
234	        begin
235	            for (int i = 0; i < TOTAL_THREADS; i++)
236	            begin
237	                load_sync_address_valid[i] <= '0;
238	                load_sync_address[i] <= '0;
239	            end
240	
241	            /*AUTORESET*/
242	            // Beginning of autoreset for uninitialized flops
243	            l2r_perf_l2_hit <= '0;
244	            l2r_perf_l2_miss <= '0;
245	            l2r_request_valid <= '0;
246	            l2r_store_sync_success <= '0;
247	            // End of automatics
248	        end
249	        else
250	        begin
251	            // A fill and cache hit cannot occur at the same time.
252	            assert(!l2t_l2_fill || !cache_hit);
253	
254	            // Make sure there isn't a hit on more than one way
255	            assert(!l2t_request_valid || $onehot0(hit_way_oh));
256	
257	            l2r_request_valid <= l2t_request_valid;
258	
259	            if (l2t_request_valid && (cache_hit || l2t_l2_fill))
260	            begin
261	                // Track synchronized load/stores
262	                unique case (l2t_request.packet_type)
263	                    L2REQ_LOAD_SYNC:
264	                    begin
265	                        load_sync_address[request_sync_slot] <= {l2t_request.address.tag, l2t_request.address.set_idx};
266	                        load_sync_address_valid[request_sync_slot] <= 1;
267	                    end
268	
269	                    L2REQ_STORE,
270	                    L2REQ_STORE_SYNC:
271	                    begin
272	                        // Don't invalidate if the sync store is not successful. Otherwise
273	                        // threads can livelock.
274	                        if (l2t_request.packet_type == L2REQ_STORE || can_store_sync)
275	                        begin
276	                            // Invalidate
277	                            for (int entry_idx = 0; entry_idx < TOTAL_THREADS; entry_idx++)
278	                            begin
279	                                if (load_sync_address[entry_idx] == {l2t_request.address.tag, l2t_request.address.set_idx})
280	                                    load_sync_address_valid[entry_idx] <= 0;
281	                            end
282	                        end
283	                    end
284	
285	                    default:
286	                        ;
287	                endcase
288	
289	                l2r_store_sync_success <= can_store_sync;
290	            end
291	            else
292	                l2r_store_sync_success <= 0;
293	
294	            // Perf events
295	            l2r_perf_l2_miss <= hit_or_miss && !(|hit_way_oh);
296	            l2r_perf_l2_hit <= hit_or_miss && |hit_way_oh;
297	        end
298	    end
299	endmodule
300

Now let me write the lecture. This is an advanced topic so I need to be precise but also build intuition. Let me think about the LL/SC mechanism in Nyuzi:

**LL/SC (Load-Linked / Store-Conditional) overview:**
- LL/SC is a lock-free atomic primitive. load_sync (LL) loads a value and "links" (reserves) the address. store_sync (SC) writes only if no other write occurred to that address since the LL; otherwise it fails.
- In Nyuzi, MEM_SYNC is used for both load_sync and store_sync (the load/store bit distinguishes them).

**The Nyuzi implementation is unusual:**
1. Both load_sync and store_sync go all the way to the L2 cache (they don't just use L1). The first load_sync is treated as a cache miss so it sends to L2 to "register" the address.
2. The L2 cache tracks per-thread (actually per {core,id} slot) the linked address. 
3. The "reservation" granularity is a cache line.
4. When any store (sync or normal) writes to a line that matches a reserved address, ALL reservations for that line are invalidated (this prevents livelock).
5. store_sync succeeds only if this thread still has a valid reservation for that address (can_store_sync).
6. The result of store_sync (success/fail as 0/1) is written back to the destination register.

**Two-pass execution:**
Both load_sync and store_sync execute in two passes through the pipeline:
- load_sync: first pass = treat as miss, send L2REQ_LOAD_SYNC to L2 (register address + fetch data), suspend thread and rollback. L2 responds with data + latches address. Second pass = now data may be in L1 cache (cache_hit allowed because dd_load_sync_pending is set), load returns the value normally.
- store_sync: first pass = enqueue store request, suspend thread and rollback (always, even if buffer has space). The store goes to L2 as L2REQ_STORE_SYNC. L2 responds with sync_success. Second pass = pick up the sync_success result, write back to dest register.

The `dd_load_sync_pending` and `sq_store_sync_pending` bits track "first or second pass" and also gate interrupts (so an interrupt can't sneak in between the two passes and corrupt state).

**membar:**
- CACHE_MEMBAR. Acts like a store in rollback logic but doesn't enqueue anything. It waits until all pending stores finish. 
- In l1_store_queue, membar causes rollback when there's a valid pending store that hasn't gotten a response yet (lines 168-170). So the thread is suspended until all stores drain.
- This provides a memory barrier / ordering guarantee: stores before membar complete (reach L2 / become visible) before instructions after membar.

Let me also clarify: the interrupt gating is important because the two-pass nature means state is held across the rollback. An interrupt between passes would corrupt it.

Now, one subtle point about the rollback for store_sync: line 162-163, `if (dd_store_sync) rollback[thread_idx] = !restarted_sync_request;`. So on the first store_sync, it always rolls back (suspends), waits for the L2 response. When the response arrives (response_received set), restarted_sync_request becomes true, so the second pass does NOT roll back — instead it clears the entry and the result is read.

Let me verify the "two pass" for store_sync:
- First pass: dd_store_sync=1, pending_stores not valid (or just being created). update_store_entry fires (since store_requested and !restarted). It sets valid=1, sync=1, request_sent=0. Also rollback is set (dd_store_sync && !restarted_sync_request → 1). So thread_waiting<=1. The entry gets sent to L2.
- L2 responds: got_response_this_entry=1, sync is set → response_received<=1, sync_success<=value. The entry stays valid (because sync). thread_waiting cleared via sq_wake_bitmap (got_response && thread_waiting).
- Thread re-executes store_sync (second pass, PC was rolled back). Now restarted_sync_request = valid && response_received && sync = 1. So rollback = !restarted_sync_request = 0 (no rollback). And update_store_entry = store_requested && ... && !restarted_sync_request = 0. In the store_requested_this_entry block, restarted_sync_request is true → valid<=0 (clears entry). The result sq_store_sync_success (which was latched = sync_success) is read by writeback.

Good, that's the two-pass mechanism.

Now for load_sync two-pass (in dcache_data_stage):
- First pass: sync_access_req=1, cached_load_req=1. cache_hit = |way_hit_oh && (!sync || dd_load_sync_pending) && tlb_hit. Since dd_load_sync_pending is 0 on first pass, cache_hit=0 → treated as miss. dd_cache_miss=1, dd_cache_miss_sync=1. dd_load_sync_pending toggled to 1. Thread suspended + rolled back. Request goes to L2 as L2REQ_LOAD_SYNC.
- L2 latches the address (load_sync_address) and responds with data, fills L1.
- Thread re-executes load_sync (second pass). Now dd_load_sync_pending=1, so cache_hit can be true (if data in L1). Load returns normally. dd_load_sync_pending toggled back to 0.

There's a note: if evicted before second pass, retry (it'll miss again, re-register). Also load_sync returns the loaded data value (not success/fail). It's store_sync that returns success/fail.

Now let me also confirm: load_sync destination register gets the loaded value (normal load path). store_sync destination register gets 0/1 success (writeback_stage line 468).

Let me write this up now. I want to make it pedagogically clear.

Let me also note the memory ordering semantics: Nyuzi is relaxed. membar provides the ordering fence. Stores go through the store queue and eventually reach L2. membar ensures all prior stores have been acknowledged (response received) before subsequent memory ops.

Actually, let me re-read the membar semantics carefully. From l1_store_queue comments (lines 24-27): "A memory barrier request waits until all pending store requests finish. It acts like a store in terms of rollback logic, but doesn't enqueue anything."

And the rollback logic for membar (lines 168-170): `else if (membar_requested_this_entry && pending_stores[thread_idx].valid && !got_response_this_entry) rollback[thread_idx] = 1;`

So: when a membar is requested and there's still a valid pending store (that hasn't been responded to this cycle), the thread is rolled back (suspended). It keeps getting retried until all pending stores have been drained (each gets a response). Once the last pending store gets its response in the same cycle, `got_response_this_entry` makes it not roll back, and since membar doesn't enqueue anything, it just completes.

Wait, but membar only checks `pending_stores[thread_idx].valid`. There's only ONE pending store entry per thread (the struct is indexed by thread, single entry each). So per thread there's at most one outstanding store. So membar just waits for that one pending store to drain. That's the barrier.

Hmm, actually since there's a single entry per thread, the store queue can only hold one outstanding store per thread anyway. So membar waits for the current pending store (if any) to complete.

OK. Now let me also double check: does membar also wait for loads? No — it only waits for stores (pending_stores). Loads that miss go through the load miss queue, not the store queue. So membar is primarily a store-store (and store visibility) barrier.

This makes sense for the spinlock use case: you want your store (e.g., writing data) to be visible before you release the lock (another store). And you may want a membar after acquiring. But Nyuzi's membar only drains the store queue.

Good. Now writing.

I want to explain why each value should be 10: There are NUM_SLOTS=512 slots. Total writes = NUM_SLOTS*10 = 5120. Each of 4 threads cycles through, atomically grabbing the next index and atomically incrementing that slot. Each slot gets incremented until the shared counter reaches 5120. Each slot gets exactly 10 increments (since index wraps mod 512 and total is 5120 = 512*10). So every slot should end at 10. If atomicity were broken, two threads could read same value and both write back, losing an increment → some slot != 10. That's the verification.

Wait let me re-read. `li s0, (NUM_SLOTS * 10)` → s0 = 5120. The loop:

- cmpge_i s4, s3, s0 → if s3 >= 5120, done
- and s3, s3, NUM_SLOTS-1 → s3 mod 512
- shl s3, s3, 2 → *4 (byte offset)
- add s3, s3, s2 → array base + offset
- sync_fetch_and_increment s4, s3 → increment that slot (s4 ignored)
- loop

So current_index goes 0,1,2,...,5119 (5120 total increments across 4 threads, atomically). Each index i (0..5119) maps to slot (i mod 512). So slot k gets incremented for i = k, k+512, k+1024, ..., k+4608 → that's 10 times (k=0: 0,512,...,4608 = 10 values; since 5120/512 = 10). So each slot = 10. The test reads 512 dwords (the array) and checks each == 10. 

If atomicity fails: two threads both read current_index=N, both compute slot=N mod 512, both increment → one increment lost from current_index (so total < 5120, but the test doesn't check that directly) AND the slot would get... actually both increment the same slot so slot gets +2 but current_index only +1. Hmm, the counting is complex. The key invariant: if perfectly atomic, each slot = 10 exactly. The test verifies all slots = 10. Any lost update would make some slot != 10. Good.

Now let me write. I'll make the spinlock practice use the macro pattern.

For a spinlock:
```
# Acquire: spin until we atomically set the lock from 0 to 1
1:  load_sync s1, (lock_addr)   # LL: read lock, reserve address
    bnz s1, 1b                   # if already locked (nonzero), retry
    move s2, 1
    store_sync s2, (lock_addr)   # SC: try to write 1
    bz s2, 1b                    # if SC failed, retry
# ... critical section ...
# Release:
    move s2, 0
    store_32 s2, (lock_addr)     # ordinary store to release
```

This is the classic LL/SC spinlock. I'll present this as the practice.

Now, the note about store_sync failure/rollback: When two threads contend, both do load_sync (both reserve the same line at L2). The first store_sync to reach L2 succeeds (can_store_sync true for it), and its store invalidates ALL reservations for that line (lines 269-283). The second thread's store_sync arrives, but its reservation is now invalid (load_sync_address_valid=0), so can_store_sync=false → store fails → store_sync writes 0 to dest reg → branch back to retry.

At the L1 level, each store_sync always does a round trip to L2 (two-pass). The "rollback" at L1 store queue is the suspend-and-replay mechanism, not a failure indicator. The actual success/fail comes from L2's can_store_sync, returned as l2rsp status.

Actually, looking at L269-283: it's `L2REQ_STORE, L2REQ_STORE_SYNC:` — so both regular stores and sync stores invalidate matching reservations. Good — this is important: a regular store to the reserved line also breaks the reservation.

Let me write the file now.

Let me be precise about the formula. I'll write the SC success condition:
\[ \text{store\_sync 成功} \iff \text{valid}[\text{slot}] \wedge \text{address}[\text{slot}] = \text{target} \]
where slot = {core, thread}.

And note the livelock prevention: any store to the matched line invalidates ALL threads' reservations for that line, so only one contender can win per round.

.macro start_all_threads
                li s0, 0xffffffff
                setcr s0, CR_RESUME_THREAD
.endm

                getcr s0, CR_CURRENT_THREAD
                move s1, 1
                shl s1, s1, s0
                setcr s1, CR_SUSPEND_THREAD
1:              b 1b
.endm

.macro halt_all_threads
                li s0, 0xffffffff
                setcr s0, CR_SUSPEND_THREAD
1:              b 1b
.endm

// Print a null terminated string pointer to by s0 to the serial
// port. Clobbers s0-s3.
                .align 4, 0xff
.macro print_string
                li s1, 0xffff0040       // Load address of serial registers
1:              load_u8 s2, (s0)        // Read a character
                bz s2, 3f               // If delimiter, exit
2:              load_32 s3, (s1)        // Read STATUS
                and s3, s3, 1           // Check write available bit
                bz s3, 2b               // If this is clear, busy wait
                store_32 s2, 8(s1)      // Write space available, send char
                add_i s0, s0, 1         // Increment pointer
                b 1b                    // Loop for next char
3:
.endm

// Print a character in s0. Clobbers s1, s2
.macro print_char
                li s1, 0xffff0040       // Load address of serial registers
1:              load_32 s2, (s1)        // Read STATUS
                and s2, s2, 1           // Check write available bit
